us-politics.headline: Trump Threatens Iran's Infrastructure, Warns of Wider War
us-politics.body:
Trump doubled down Monday on a Tuesday-night deadline for Iran to accept US demands, threatening strikes on civilian infrastructure if no deal is reached. He invoked God's support for the US cause and suggested America could charge tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage - a waterway OPEC+ is already planning to boost output through once it reopens. The Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and General Caine, offering no explanation.

Trump brushed off questions about potential war crimes and detailed a rescue operation for a missing F-15 airman. A former NATO commander warned Iran still holds leverage, having rejected a ceasefire proposal. Critics across the spectrum are watching whether the deadline produces diplomacy or escalation.

sources.title: Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Former NATO commander on Iran rejecting ceasefire proposal: ‘They still have cards to play’

us-politics.headline: Immigration Enforcement Battles Flare Across Multiple Fronts
us-politics.body:
Senator Mullin pressed cities with international airports to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement, warning that resistance carries consequences. In Arizona, Republicans forced a formal probe into one county's anti-ICE policies, putting the Democratic attorney general under pressure to respond. Laura Loomer claimed credit for the arrest of a former Iranian commander's niece in California, though the circumstances remain disputed.

Separately, a rare bipartisan coalition emerged over H2A visa rules - both conservatives and liberals oppose proposed changes to the agricultural guest-worker program, citing harm to farmers and workers alike.

sources.title: Mullin to cities with international airports: ‘You’ve got to partner with us’
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers
Loomer takes credit for arrest of former Iranian commander’s niece in California

us-politics.headline: Trump Attacks Supreme Court, Reshapes Policy on Multiple Fronts
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly criticized the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariffs, signaling continued friction between the White House and the judiciary. The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements tied to gender identity, reversing Biden-era policies at schools nationwide. The EPA took an early step toward rolling back methane flaring rules, drawing environmental criticism.

Defense Secretary Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and directed funding toward Hillsdale College. The moves collectively reflect a broad administrative push to reshape federal policy across defense, education, and environment simultaneously.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape as Money and Candidates Emerge
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in Senate midterm races, signaling an early and aggressive push to hold the chamber. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines backed Kurt Alme as his handpicked successor for 2026. An AI-backed super PAC claimed wins in early GOP primaries and is now expanding into multiple races.

Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin hinted at future political ambitions, saying he has more to give. Virginia Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger saw her approval ratings drop sharply amid a gerrymandering controversy. Representative Anna Luna called on two House colleagues to resign over separate conduct disputes.

sources.title: New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Luna says Gonzales, Cherfilus-McCormick ‘need to go’
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races

au-politics.headline: Trump slams Australia over Iran war, threatens journalist
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump publicly attacked Australia, Japan and South Korea for refusing to join the US military campaign against Iran, saying the allies 'didn't help'. The comments drew sharp attention in Canberra, where the government is already managing fuel supply pressures caused by the conflict - Communications Minister Michelle Rowland confirmed officials are focused on supply and are 'not considering' rationing.

Trump separately threatened to jail the journalist who broke the story of a US rescue mission in Iran, raising press freedom concerns. Back home, the war's economic ripple effects are widening - PVC prices have surged due to Middle East supply disruptions, hitting Australia's building and construction sectors. The ASX rose 2 per cent amid volatile global trading as an Iran deadline loomed.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; army reservist charged with fighting in Ukraine
Trump lashes out at Australia, Japan and South Korea for not helping in Iran war – video
Trump threatens to jail journalist who broke story of US rescue mission in Iran
Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket
Live: ASX surges 2pc after modest gains on Wall St as Iran deadline looms
Live: Government focused on supply, 'not considering' fuel rationing: Rowland
Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia

au-politics.headline: Army reservist charged for allegedly fighting in Ukraine
au-politics.body:
A South Australian army reservist has been charged after allegedly working for the Ukrainian military without authorisation, in what appears to be the first such prosecution under Australian defence law. The charge does not relate to the legality of Ukraine's cause but to the requirement that Australian Defence Force members seek approval before serving with foreign forces.

The case raises questions about how Australia manages reservists who act independently during an active international conflict. It comes as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy renewed calls for a ceasefire ahead of Orthodox Easter, with the war now directly intersecting with Australian legal and diplomatic concerns.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; army reservist charged with fighting in Ukraine
Army reservist allegedly worked for Ukraine military without authorisation
Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter

au-politics.headline: 'Adult crime, adult time' laws yet to catch a single child
au-politics.body:
Data shows no children have faced court under the new offences created by Queensland's 'adult crime, adult time' legislation in the 11 years those offences have existed. The finding undermines a central justification used to pass the laws, which were framed as a response to serious youth crime and attracted significant political debate.

Separately, the Northern Territory parliament is facing criticism over a gambling reforms bill, with opponents calling it a 'kneejerk' reaction to a Four Corners investigation rather than considered policy. Critics argue the bill was rushed and may create conflicts within the Racing and Wagering Commission. Both cases point to ongoing tension between political urgency and evidence-based lawmaking.

sources.title: No children faced court on new 'adult crime, adult time' offences in past 11 years
Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners

au-politics.headline: Reef damage mounts as environmental pressures stack up
au-politics.body:
Two separate environmental crises are threatening Australia's major reef systems. Wrecked boats have been leaching styrofoam and chemicals into the Great Barrier Reef for months, with salvage operators facing criticism over the slow response. Marine experts warn the contamination window has been far too long.

Further north, ex-Cyclone Narelle has left marine animals strewn along beaches near Ningaloo Reef, raising fears about the impact on coral spawning. The back-to-back incidents have renewed calls for stronger emergency response protocols for marine environments. Farmers are also pushing back on renewable energy policy, demanding stronger protections as large-scale projects expand across agricultural land.

sources.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches
Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months
Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy policy

world.headline: Trump Threatens Total Destruction as Iran Deal Deadline Looms
world.body:
The United States is intensifying strikes on Iran as a self-imposed deadline for a nuclear deal approaches, with Trump threatening to destroy the country 'in one night' if no agreement is reached. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed bombing runs are increasing, and Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant and a leading university have both been hit. Iran has shown little sign of capitulating.

Trump also floated charging ships for passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a move that would upend global shipping norms. A rescued US airman was recovered from a remote part of Iran during the campaign. Trump separately threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of that rescue operation.

sources.title: Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Trump's Hormuz ultimatum nears - with little indication Iran is on board
Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive

world.headline: Iran, Hezbollah and Houthis Strike Israel as Lebanon Offensive Deepens
world.body:
Iran launched strikes on Israel, with Lebanon's Hezbollah and Yemen's Houthis joining the assault in a coordinated multi-front attack. Israel has responded with renewed air strikes on Lebanon as its ground offensive there expands. In Gaza, Israeli strikes killed at least ten Palestinians near a school, with additional deaths reported in clashes involving Hamas and a rival militia.

Protesters stormed the US embassy in Tel Aviv demanding an end to the wars. Israeli soldiers also fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron. The widening conflict is driving a global energy crisis, with Asia bearing the sharpest impact and cities like Cairo facing power cuts as oil prices soar.

sources.title: Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar

world.headline: Russia Strikes Ukraine Hard; Kursk Governor Jailed Over Graft
world.body: Russia escalated strikes on Ukraine, with rescuers pulling a child from rubble after attacks hit residential areas. The bombardment comes as front-line pressure on Ukrainian forces continues. Russia's former Kursk regional governor was sentenced to prison in a corruption case linked to the Ukrainian incursion into the region last year.

sources.title: Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine

world.headline: India Death Sentences, Artemis Record, and Bannon Case Cleared
world.body: An Indian court sentenced nine police officers to death for killing detainees in custody during the Covid-19 pandemic, a rare ruling against law enforcement in the country. The US Supreme Court cleared the way for dismissal of Steve Bannon's criminal contempt case. NASA's Artemis II crew broke the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth.

sources.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth

ai.headline: OpenAI Leadership Crisis Deepens as Insiders Lose Trust
ai.body:
OpenAI insiders are openly questioning Sam Altman's leadership, with sources describing a culture of distrust around the CEO. The tension surfaces as the company pushes an ambitious economic vision - public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek - that critics may see as distraction from internal dysfunction.

Meanwhile, a group of OpenAI alumni have quietly assembled a fund approaching $100 million to back early-stage AI startups, signaling that talent and capital are already hedging away from the mothership. The exits and the skepticism together paint a picture of an organization under real strain at the top.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate Data Centers as AI Infrastructure Becomes a Target
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers, specifically targeting the Abu Dhabi facility under development in the UAE. The threats mark a notable escalation - AI infrastructure is now explicitly in the crosshairs of geopolitical adversaries.

Stargate, the $500 billion joint venture backed by OpenAI, SoftBank, and others, was already a lightning rod for scrutiny over its scale and financing. Physical threats to its facilities add a new dimension of risk that neither the AI industry nor its government partners have had to seriously plan around before.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability, Reshaping What Automation Can Do
ai.body:
Generalist's GEN-1 robotics model has reached 99% task reliability across a wide range of physical jobs - folding boxes, repairing vacuums, and more. That number matters because industrial automation has long stalled on the gap between lab performance and production-floor consistency. Closing it changes the economic calculus for manufacturers.

The result lands alongside Anthropic's expanded compute partnership with Google and Broadcom, underscoring that the hardware and model infrastructure needed to run capable physical AI is rapidly consolidating among a small number of players.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: AI Floods iTunes Charts While Workers Wait for Real Labor Data
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart simultaneously, despite not being a real person. The chart takeover is a concrete example of AI-generated content displacing human creators at scale, not in theory but in practice.

Yet as MIT Technology Review reports, the data needed to honestly assess AI's impact on employment barely exists. Policymakers and workers are navigating a major economic shift with almost no reliable signal on what jobs are actually being lost or changed - and who is bearing the cost.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

btc.headline: Bitcoin Climbs Past $70K but Options Signal More Pain Ahead
btc.body:
Bitcoin pushed back above $70,000 Monday as contrarian indicators suggested a potential bottom, but profit-taking quickly capped the rally. Strategy's aggressive accumulation - absorbing nearly three times the newly mined BTC supply - has analysts floating a path to $110,000 if demand holds.

The options market tells a different story. Traders are quietly pricing in significant downside protection, suggesting institutional hedgers are not convinced the worst is over. Geopolitics adds another variable: Trump's ultimatum to Iran briefly lifted sentiment, but its staying power remains unclear.

Analysts see the $70,000 level as a critical line. A clean break higher would shift momentum; failure to hold it keeps the bearish case alive.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran

btc.headline: Polymarket Overhauls Exchange, Drops Bridged USDC for Native Token
btc.body:
Polymarket launched a full exchange upgrade, replacing USDC.e - a bridged version of the stablecoin - with a native USDC-backed token. The move gives the prediction market platform direct control over its own order matching and settlement, reducing reliance on third-party infrastructure.

The upgrade comes as Polymarket looks to sharpen its competitive edge and tighten the link between market outcomes and on-chain truth. Taking ownership of core trading mechanics is a significant operational shift for a platform that has grown into one of crypto's most-watched prediction venues.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth

btc.headline: Dimon Warns JPMorgan Must Move Faster as Tokenization Bites
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as genuine competitive threats, pointing to the bank's own Kinexys platform as evidence the firm is moving - but warning it needs to move faster. The acknowledgment from Wall Street's most prominent banking chief marks a notable shift in tone from years of Bitcoin skepticism.

Dimon's comments land as tokenization gains real traction. Bernstein separately argued that Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double the company's stock price, citing loan growth potential. The pressure on traditional finance to adapt is no longer theoretical.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance

btc.headline: Courts Back Kalshi, Senate Eyes April Crypto Market Structure Vote
btc.body:
A US appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports prediction markets, handing the CFTC-regulated platform a second consecutive legal win against state-level enforcement. The ruling reinforces federal jurisdiction over event contracts and weakens the patchwork of state challenges that have dogged the sector.

On Capitol Hill, a Senate Banking Committee member confirmed an April timeline for a crypto market structure bill. Progress on both fronts signals a regulatory environment that is slowly - if unevenly - moving toward clearer rules for digital asset markets.

sources.title: US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for 40-metre lengths, resting only between variations — brutally effective for grip, core stability, and real-world strength

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memories, and consciousness itself
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' pushes the puzzle into genuinely unsettling territory about whether the 'you' who wakes up tomorrow is the same person who went to sleep — an hour spent here quietly rewires how you think about continuity, responsibility, and what you actually are
